title: Molecular Identification of Phytopathogenic Fungi
author: Unknown
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.shteb6n

steps.instruction: Obtain 10 discs of agar culture (1 x 1 cm) from a 7-day-old colony of target fungus grown on PDA or another culture medium. Transferred them into 250-ml Erlenmeyer flasks containing 50 ml of nutrient broth (NB) medium Incubate at 28°C on an orbital shaker (100 rpm) for three or four days Collect mycelia by filtration Frozen mycelia at -80°C until use, or frozen mycelia at -80°C for 2 hours and lyophilized and store until use (optional).

Resuspend fungal mycelia in 1000 µL of sterile distilled water and add two 5-mm sterile porcelain beads, vortex at full speed for 3–5 min. Freeze at –80°C for 10 min. Defrost (3 min, at 65°C in a water bath) and vortex at full speed again for 3 min. Transfer 300 µL of cell suspension to a new 2-mL microcentrifuge tube. Add 800 µL of lysing buffer. Incubate at 65°C for 30 min, with occasional inversion. Cool the mixture to room temperature (RT). Add 500 µL of chloroform and invert gently for 10 min. Centrifuge for 10 min at 15,682g at RT. Transfer top aqueous layer to a new 2-mL microcentrifuge tube Add 700 µL of chilled isopropanol. Mix by very gentle inversion Incubate for 10 min at –80°C Centrifuge for 5 min at 15,682g at RT. Discard the supernatant. Wash pellet with 500 µL of 70% ethanol Dry under vacuum centrifugation (optional). Resuspend the dry pellet in 50 µL of TE buffer

Amplified the internal transcribed spacer region, including the 5.8S rDNA, using universal primers ITS1 (5’- TCCGTAGGTGAACCTGCGG-3’) and ITS4 (5’- TCCTCCGCTTATTGATATGC-3’) Carried out PCR reaction in 25 µL, consisting of 25 ng of DNA, 1× PCR buffer (Invitrogen), 0.20 mM of each dNTP (Invitrogen), 1.5 mM MgCl 2 , 1 µM of each primer and 1U Taq polymerase (Invitrogen). Perform DNA amplification in a thermal cycler as GeneAmp 9700 DNA Thermal Cycler (Perkin-Elmer) Amplification program consisted of an initial denaturing step at 95° for 1 min, followed by 30 cycles of 1 min at 94°C, 1 min at 58°C and 1 min at 72°C, and a final extension step of 7 min at 72°C. PCR product can be visualized by electrophoresis in 1.5% (w/v) agarose gels and ethidium bromide staining. The band size obtained will be between 500 and 800 base pairs, depending on the filamentous fungus species studied. The quantity and quality of DNA obtained must be determined. DNA quantified spectrophotometrically and DNA quality by calculating the A260/A280 ratio.
PCR products obtained must be purified and sequenced. Note: In our case, we send all our PCR products to Macrogen Inc. Korea for sequencing
Fungal sequences should be alignment and edited using the Bio Edit Sequence Alignment program.
